headline: Kedah Rice Fields Lose 8,400 Hectares to Development Since 2015
context: MADA reported that 8,400 hectares of designated rice paddies in the Muda agricultural zone were converted to non-agricultural use between 2015 and 2024.

cards.sub: Actual self-sufficiency fell to 63% in 2024 while 8,400 hectares of prime paddy land were rezoned for development.

cards.big: MADA's designated agricultural zone shrank 11% in one decade.
cards.sub: State governments have authority to rezone agricultural land. Federal food security targets are advisory only.

cards.big: Development yields RM4.2M per hectare versus RM8,500 per hectare for rice.
cards.sub: The 500:1 economic ratio makes rezoning irresistible without federal intervention.

cards.big: Rice imports cost Malaysia RM3.8B in 2024.
cards.sub: Global rice prices rose 28% in 2023-2024. Import dependency is a food security vulnerability.

cards.big: We are converting rice land to condos while spending RM3.8B importing rice.
Food security requires land. If agricultural zones can be rezoned by states without federal consent, national food security targets are structurally unenforceable.
